title: WED-15-I7

'''WED-15-I7'''<ref name="SWE">''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> was a [[WSB-15 sabotage droid]], a [[Class four droid/Legends|fourth-degree]] military [[Saboteur/Legends|sabotage]] [[Droid/Legends|droid]] model physically identical to the [[WED Treadwell repair droid/Legends|WED Treadwell repair droid]]. WED-15-I7 was individually physically identical to the [[WED-15 Septoid Treadwell/Legends|WED-15 Septoid Treadwell]]. The [[Droid/Legends|droid]] had eight [[Manipulator arm/Legends|manipulator arms]], along with ten treaded [[wheel]]s at its base. Possessing [[Color/Legends|gray]] plating, WED-15-I7 had two red panels at the base above the treaded wheels, along with a pair of gray [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]].<ref name="Threats" />

By [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],<ref name="NEC">{{CCG|set=A New Hope Limited|cardname=WED15-I7 "Septoid" Droid}} is set around the [[Time/Legends|time]] of {{Film|IV}}, the events of which are dated to [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] by ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> WED-15-I7 served the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] as a [[Maintenance droid/Legends|maintenance droid]] with a "Septoid" nickname<ref name="CCG" />—both characteristic of the WED-15 Septoid Treadwell model the individual WSB-15 was physically identical to.<ref name="FF1">{{FactFile|16|WED 1-4|WED Treadwell Repair Droid}}</ref> A fiercely loyal maintenance droid, WED-15-I7 specialized in extending Imperial resources' effective operational lives.<ref name="CCG" />

==Behind the scenes==
WED-15-I7 was created for the [[1996]] ''[[A New Hope Limited]]'' set of the [[Decipher]] [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]].<ref name="CCG" /> Of note, the [[October 5]], [[1998]]<ref name="Amazon">{{DK|url=us/9780789434814-star-wars-the-visual-dictionary|text=Star Wars: The Visual Dictionary|nolive=1}}</ref> ''[[Star Wars: The Visual Dictionary]]'' used the same image to represent another droid, [[WED 15 Septoid 2]].<ref name="VD">''[[Star Wars: The Visual Dictionary]]''</ref> While the droid was called "WED15-I7" by ''A New Hope Limited'',<ref name="CCG" /> [[June 30]], 1998's<ref name="San Diego Comic-Con Exclusives">{{ThisWeek|-BZX-WxjhFE|San Diego Comic-Con Exclusives, Brand New LEGO Art, and More!|sw_url=video/san-diego-comic-con-exclusives-brand-new-lego-art-and-more}}</ref> ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' would call it "WED-15-I7."<ref name="SWE" /> This article treats the latter name as correct.

Discrepancies also exist with WED-15-I7's class. With ''A New Hope Limited'' describing it as a maintenance droid nicknamed "Septoid," the ''Star Wars Encyclopedia'' specifically identified it as a [[WED Treadwell repair droid/Legends|WED model droid]].<ref name="CCG" /> [[2002]]'s [[The Official Star Wars Fact File 16|16th issue]] of ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File]]'' magazine would picture WED-15-I7 and identify the droid as a WED-15 Septoid Treadwell.<ref name="FF1">{{FactFile|16|WED 1-4|WED Treadwell Repair Droid}}</ref> However, in [[March]] [[2008]], [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s [[roleplaying game]] sourcebook ''[[Threats of the Galaxy]]'' contradicted the previous materials by including a picture of WED-15-I7 in its entry for WSB-15 sabotage droids.<ref name="Threats" /> Although the [[December 9]], [[2008]]<ref name="release date">{{SWArchive|url=vault/books/news20080926.html|text=Inside ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia''}}</ref> ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' would again describe WED-15-I7 as a Septoid and a maintenance droid,<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|III|324|WED-15-I7}}</ref> this article treats the droid's WSB-15 sabotage droid class as correct.
